sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Mr. James is a 64-year-old male presenting with a 5-month history of constant pain in his left shoulder and elbow, which started after a fall 5 months ago while going up stairs.
The pain is worse with lifting, particularly when lifting more than 15-20 pounds, and has been exacerbated by his weightlifting routine at the gym.
He has not taken any pain medication but has tried icing and stopping exercise, which has provided some relief.
He denies numbness or tingling in the affected area.
Past medical history: Not mentioned.
Review of systems: Not mentioned.
Current medications: Not mentioned.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's left shoulder and elbow were tender to palpation, with no warmth, erythema, or deformity.
The Hawkins-Kennedy and Neer's tests were positive.
The proximal and distal upper extremity strength were normal, and the median, radial, and ulnar sensation were intact.
The patient had abduction to 90 degrees and a normal empty can test.
Test Results: Not mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is experiencing impingement syndrome of the left shoulder.
Problem list: Shoulder impingement syndrome; Elbow pain; Status: active.
Plan:
I prescribed physical therapy with Dr. Martha Rivera two days per week.
I recommended that the patient continue to ice the shoulder and rest as much as possible.
I will obtain a referral for physical therapy, and the patient will be contacted within the next day or two to schedule an appointment.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me or contact our office if his symptoms worsen or do not improve with physical therapy.
